_note: Acceptance criteria for the live performance-acceptance harness (Context B; cabal executable perf-acceptance, make perf-acceptance). Version-controlled on purpose: moving the bar is an explicit, reviewed act. Two overheads are budgeted per package. `defaultBudgetMs`/`perPackageBudgetMs` bound the full-packument work-per-request (decode -> project -> rule sweep -> merge -> served-body assembly with the fused rewrite -> re-serialise) over a single live packument. `defaultSingleVersionBudgetMs`/`perPackageSingleVersionBudgetMs` bound the single-version selective decode the cold tarball gate consults to serve one package version (its latest) — the per-package overhead a whole-document decode dominates and a selective decode does not, tracked on its own so a single-version improvement stays visible. A breach of either leg reds the (non-required) check to prompt a human decision — code regression vs reality outgrew the budget — and never blocks merge. These bounds are calibrated from a first real run with deliberately generous headroom (roughly 3x or more over the measured overhead) to absorb shared-runner noise and real-world packument growth; tighten or relax them as the real runs accrue — that is what version-controlling them is for. See docs/architecture/performance.md.
